mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid labor market deterioration exceeding a 0.3 percentage point monthly increase in the unemployment rate triggers risk-off repricing, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power for weekly drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based demand expansion, which supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling window, or earnings revisions do not correlate positively with the composite demand index.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its 10-year trend while inflation remains elevated, supply bottlenecks compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Margin compression does not materialize when INDPRO and CPIAUCSL are simultaneously elevated, or forward returns remain positive despite the identified supply-inflation mismatch.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations and corporate investment, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Equity valuations do not compress when rates breach the threshold, or forward quarterly returns are statistically indistinguishable from periods with lower policy rates.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reduces the equity risk premium through improved earnings visibility, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The signal shows no directional consistency over a 5-year rolling window, or forward weekly returns are flat regardless of GDP growth trajectory.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The composite labor signal fails to predict monthly equity returns, or payroll growth decouples from unemployment stability without impacting market direction.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding grows faster than real GDP while housing permits decline, demand is being propped up by debt, signaling an imminent earnings recession and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Debt-fueled consumption does not precede earnings contractions, or forward quarterly returns remain positive despite the identified demand-debt divergence.
